reference: Siclari, Baird, Perogamvros, Bernardi, LaRocque, Riedner, Boly, Postle, Tononi 2017 Nat Neurosci 20(6):872-878 (DOI 10.1038/nn.4545)
reason: Requires user wearing 16-ch EEG during sleep over multiple nights with serial-awakening dream-report protocol; cannot be auto-executed by N-21-N agent

hardware.rationale: Siclari 2017 identified a posterior cortical hot zone (parieto-occipital) where high-frequency power increase / low-frequency decrease predicted dream presence on awakening; hot zone is independent of frontal regions

protocol_steps: Step 1: Pre-sleep baseline 5min eyes-closed resting EEG (compute LZc as reference)
Step 2: Don EEG cap, sleep with cap on (target 5-7 nights)
Step 3: Serial awakenings via random-interval alarm (every 30-90 min during NREM/REM windows)
Step 4: On each awakening, immediate verbal report (recorded): 'Were you dreaming? If yes, describe content, was it perceptual/abstract/none?'
Step 5: Mark awakening timestamp in EEG ledger
Step 6: For each 20s window pre-awakening, compute: (a) high-gamma 25-50Hz power in posterior channels, (b) low-delta 1-4Hz power in posterior channels, (c) LZc per channel
Step 7: Classify reports: dream-experience (DE) vs no-experience (NE) vs forgotten (NF)
Step 8: Test hypothesis: posterior high-gamma > low-delta predicts DE vs NE with d > 0.5

analysis_pipeline.preprocessing: 1-50Hz bandpass, ICA artifact removal (already implemented in state/clm_eeg_*_audit/)
analysis_pipeline.stats: mixed-effects logistic regression: report (DE/NE) ~ posterior_gamma + posterior_delta + (1|night)

expected_pass_criterion: AUROC of posterior gamma/delta ratio for DE-vs-NE >= 0.65 (Siclari paper reports ~0.71 with 256ch HD-EEG)
limitations_vs_paper: No PSG (polysomnography) — sleep stage from EEG alone, less reliable
next_action: Schedule with user a 5-night sleep recording window; agent will pre-register protocol JSON before user begins
